title: Aureline break-glass event contract
description: Boundary schema for break_glass_event_record, the reviewable audit row produced whenever a high-risk freeze, revocation, trust-root, or emergency-policy action is invoked under the audited_single_responder_containment break-glass profile in artifacts/governance/signing_quorum.yaml rather than under the full default quorum. One row per invocation carries the signing-quorum action ref, the break-glass profile ref, the invoking actor identity, the admitted reason and urgency, the affected scope (subjects, install-profile-card refs, channel classes, deployment profiles), the produced emergency-action and revocation ids, the triage / advisory / incident / post-incident / release-evidence / support-export / admin-export cross-links, invocation and must-close-by timestamps, a frozen lifecycle state (invoked_pending_reconciliation, reconciled_with_retrospective_cosign, superseded_by_signed_action, expired_without_reconciliation, withdrawn_invalid_invocation), the retrospective cosign block that closes the invocation, and redaction metadata. Raw human identifiers, raw chat handles, raw pager ids, raw signing material, raw quorum-signature bytes, and raw support attachments never cross this boundary; the record carries opaque refs, typed vocabulary, and redaction-safe summary fields only. The private_triage_workspace_packet_record.break_glass_refs.audit_row_ref, advisory_record.decision_history refs, emergency_action_record.history_links refs, release-evidence decision-history refs, support-export packet decision-history refs, admin-export decision-history refs, and post-incident review rows all resolve to the same break_glass_event_id rather than re-keying. Adding a new reason_class, lifecycle_state, actor_class, cosigner_role_class, cosigner_forum_class, history_link slot, redaction_class, or scope class is additive-minor and requires a break_glass_event_schema_version bump; repurposing an existing value is breaking and requires a new decision row co-signed by security_trust_review and release_council.

break_glass_event_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for this break-glass event export. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

record_kind.description: Discriminator. Every break-glass event carries record_kind = break_glass_event_record so parity audits, admin exports, and support packets can validate payloads mechanically.

opaque_id.description: Opaque, stable id safe to log, safe on RPC, safe in manifests / journal entries / support bundles / claim manifests / release-evidence packets. Raw bundle bytes, raw signing material, raw quorum signatures, raw human identifiers, raw chat handles, and raw pager ids never appear here.

monotonic_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp produced from a monotonic clock source.

reviewable_sentence.description: Short, reviewable sentence describing posture, mitigation, or close-out guidance. Raw secret material, raw incident notes, and raw exploit payloads MUST NOT appear.

signing_quorum_action_ref.description: Frozen action-ref vocabulary. Mirrors artifacts/governance/signing_quorum.yaml#actions[] for the rows whose break_glass_profile is audited_single_responder_containment. An invocation that names any other action id is non-conforming (break_glass_for_forbidden_action) and MUST be rejected.

break_glass_profile_ref.description: Frozen break-glass-profile ref. Mirrors artifacts/governance/signing_quorum.yaml#break_glass_profiles[]. At this revision the single admitted profile is audited_single_responder_containment; adding a new profile is additive-minor and lands this enum value plus the governance decision row in the same change.

actor_class.description: Frozen invoking-actor vocabulary. Mirrors the role classes named in signing_quorum.yaml quorum_profiles plus org_admin and support_operator for self-hosted and support-owned break-glass paths. end_user is deliberately not admitted: break-glass is an operator action.

actor_role_class.description: Frozen actor-role vocabulary. Names which standing role the invoker held when they invoked break-glass. Mirrors the reviewer-depth and backup-owner roles in docs/governance/maintainer_coverage_policy.md.

actor_account_ref.description: Opaque ref to the operator account or service principal the invocation is attributed to. Raw human identifiers (email, login handle, real name, chat handle, pager id, hostname) MUST NOT appear.

invoking_actor.description: Invoking-actor envelope. Names who invoked break-glass under stable opaque refs so admin-export, support-export, and post-incident review rows agree on attribution without leaking raw identifiers.

reason_class.description: Frozen admitted-cause vocabulary. Mirrors artifacts/governance/signing_quorum.yaml break_glass_profiles[audited_single_responder_containment].allowed_when. A record that resolves to any other cause is non-conforming (break_glass_cause_not_admitted). The forbidden-cause list (stable / LTS promotion, widening claim scope, permanent signer roster change, audit-history mutation) is enforced by the signing_quorum_action_ref enum above and by §4.2 of docs/security/high_risk_control_quorum.md.

urgency_class.description: Frozen urgency vocabulary. Re-exported by value from schemas/security/emergency_action_record.schema.json so the break-glass event and the produced emergency action cannot drift on urgency.

security_subject_kind.description: Frozen subject-kind vocabulary. Mirrors schemas/security/emergency_action_record.schema.json#/$defs/security_subject_kind so the break-glass event and the produced emergency action resolve the same subject family.

affected_subject_ref.description: Typed subject ref. Raw subject bodies never appear here.

affected_subjects.description: Typed refs to every subject the invocation affects. An invocation MUST name at least one subject.

affected_install_profile_card_refs.description: Opaque refs into artifacts/release/install_topology_matrix.yaml. An invocation MUST name at least one install-profile card so support exports and admin exports can project scope without re-keying.

affected_exact_build_identity_refs.description: Opaque refs into schemas/build/exact_build_identity.schema.json. Free-text 'version 1.x' is non-conforming. Empty admissible for scope classes where the invocation affects a channel pointer or trust root rather than a specific build.

affected_channel_classes.description: Release channel classes the invocation narrows. Drawn from the exact-build identity model's vocabulary.

affected_deployment_profile_scope.description: Deployment profiles the invocation applies to. An invocation MUST name at least one profile so mirror / offline / air-gapped consumers can resolve scope.

break_glass_scope.description: Scope envelope shared with the emergency-action model. A break-glass event and the emergency-action or revocation it produced MUST resolve the same install-profile cards and deployment-profile set; drift is non-conforming.

lifecycle_state_class.description: Frozen lifecycle-state vocabulary. See docs/security/high_risk_control_quorum.md §6 for the admitted transitions. A record MUST NOT move between closed states; corrections are minted as new events.

retrospective_cosign_profile_ref.description: Frozen retrospective-quorum-profile ref. Mirrors artifacts/governance/signing_quorum.yaml#quorum_profiles[] for the profiles admissible as close-out signoff. At this revision every admitted break-glass action closes out against two_person_cross_forum_emergency_control; the other values are reserved for future action rows whose default quorum is wider.

cosigner_role_class.description: Frozen cosigner-role vocabulary. Mirrors the required_roles named on signing_quorum.yaml#quorum_profiles[].

cosigner_forum_class.description: Frozen cosigner-forum vocabulary. Mirrors the required_forums named on signing_quorum.yaml#quorum_profiles[].

cosigner_account_refs.description: Opaque refs to the distinct accounts that cosigned close-out. The invoking_actor.actor_account_ref MUST NOT appear in this list; author-only close-out is non-conforming.

cosigner_role_classes.description: Roles the cosigners held. Must include every required_role from the named quorum profile.

cosigner_forum_classes.description: Forums that ratified the close-out. Must include every required_forum from the named quorum profile.

retrospective_cosign.description: Retrospective cosign envelope. Populated on reconciled_with_retrospective_cosign or superseded_by_signed_action; null on open, expired, or withdrawn events.

history_links.description: Durable history-link set preserved by release-evidence, support-export, admin-export, incident-workspace, and post-incident review surfaces. Every surface quoting the event cites the same id rather than minting a local label.

redaction_class.description: Frozen redaction-class vocabulary. Mirrors schemas/security/advisory_record.schema.json. release_public is the only class that may appear verbatim in public release notes or About / Help dialogs. private_triage_only is admissible only when the event is still carried inside a named private-triage workspace scope.

break_glass_event_id.description: Opaque, stable id for the invocation. Stable for the life of the event; every downstream advisory, emergency-action, revocation, release-evidence packet, support export, admin export, and post-incident review cites this id rather than re-keying.

title.description: Short, reviewable title. Never carries raw secret material, raw identifiers, or raw exploit payload.

related_emergency_action_refs.description: Opaque refs to emergency_action_record ids produced or advanced by the invocation. Empty admissible only when the invocation produced a revocation_record instead; the allOf gate below enforces the pairing.

related_revocation_refs.description: Opaque refs to revocation_record ids produced or advanced by the invocation. Empty admissible only when the invocation produced an emergency_action_record instead; the allOf gate below enforces the pairing.

related_advisory_refs.description: Opaque refs to advisory_record ids the invocation affected. Empty admissible when the invocation preceded advisory drafting; a linked advisory is still added as a history_links decision row once drafted.

related_private_triage_packet_ref.description: Opaque ref to the private_triage_workspace_packet_record that owns the incident during response. The triage packet's break_glass_refs.audit_row_ref MUST equal this record's break_glass_event_id.

invoked_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp the invocation took effect. Set once on invocation and never mutated; corrections land as new events.

must_close_by.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp by which the invocation MUST move to a closed lifecycle state. At most 24 hours after invoked_at per the audited_single_responder_containment profile. Raising the ceiling is breaking.

closed_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp the invocation closed. Null while lifecycle_state is invoked_pending_reconciliation; non-null on every closed state.

retrospective_cosign.description: Retrospective cosign block. Null while lifecycle_state is invoked_pending_reconciliation, expired_without_reconciliation, or withdrawn_invalid_invocation; non-null on reconciled_with_retrospective_cosign and superseded_by_signed_action.

temporary_mitigation_note.description: Short, reviewable sentence describing what the break-glass step did in product terms (what was frozen, disabled, paused, or narrowed).

reconciliation_plan_note.description: Short, reviewable sentence describing how the invocation will be closed out (retrospective cosign, superseding signed action, expiry correction, or withdrawal).

minted_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp the record was first minted. Equal to or slightly after invoked_at; never earlier.

allOf.description: The invocation MUST produce at least one emergency_action_record or revocation_record ref. An event that names neither cannot be projected as containment.
reconciled_with_retrospective_cosign requires closed_at and a non-null retrospective_cosign block.
superseded_by_signed_action requires closed_at and a non-null retrospective_cosign block naming the quorum the superseding action carried.
expired_without_reconciliation requires closed_at and forbids a retrospective_cosign block (the invocation closed without quorum coverage).
withdrawn_invalid_invocation requires closed_at and forbids a retrospective_cosign block.

break_glass_event_record.description: Break-glass event record. One row per admitted single-responder invocation. The record ties the invocation to its signing-quorum action row, its admitted cause, its produced emergency-action / revocation ids, and its retrospective close-out, so release, security, support, admin-export, and post-incident review surfaces resolve one stable break_glass_event_id rather than inventing surface-local audit labels.
